hints.modelUsageTrend: Model usage trend: trailing 28-day average volume vs the prior 28-day average (monthly-level smoothing that dilutes single-week holiday noise). >+3% (roughly ≥+47% annualized growth) → strong demand · loose; <−3% (sustained decline in the monthly average) → weakening demand · oversupply warning · tight. Source: openrouter.ai/rankings — a single-aggregator proxy subject to share noise.
hints.capexYoY: Rule: hyperscaler (MSFT/AMZN/GOOGL/META) rolling-4Q capex YoY >+10% → loose vote (investment boom); <0% → tight vote (capex contraction · oversupply warning — any tight link in the trio makes AI supply/demand tight). 2nd layer of the cash-flow trio (investment side): model providers buy cloud compute
hints.semiIpYoy: Rule: semiconductor output YoY >+5% → loose vote; <0% → tight vote. Semi output is the supply-side tail of the AI supply/demand cash-flow trio (usage → cloud capex → semi output). Trio consensus rule: any link contracting → tight (oversupply warning); the whole chain rising → loose (undersupply); otherwise neutral.
hints.rate: Judged by the direction of the latest FOMC decision: a hike (incl. gradual 25bp) → tight, held until the next decision; a cut or hold → loose. A single adjustment ≥50bp (either direction) additionally triggers the reactive adjustment lock, forcing full defense.
hints.balanceSheet: Balance sheet change (WoW): expansion >0.25% (QE) → loose vote; contraction >0.25% (QT) does not read tight on its own — it only blocks a loose rating (backtest: markets rose in most QT years); in between → pause.
hints.sahm: Rule: Sahm value ≥0.5% → early recession, triggers the Sahm lock forcing full defense (<0.5% does not trigger). Unlock: rate down to ≤0.25% (zero-rate zone — unconditional unlock, even with the Sahm value still elevated, not subject to the minimum lock age); or a <50bp small rate adjustment — requiring both that the Sahm value has fallen back below 0.5% that day (trigger gone) and the lock is at least 60 days old (minimum lock age)
hints.yieldCurve: 10-year minus 3-month US Treasury yield. Normally positive (lending longer should earn more); negative = inversion. Why it works: (1) the 10-year yield reflects expected future short rates, so inversion means the bond market is pricing in "the Fed will cut hard = recession"; (2) banks borrow short and lend long — inversion makes lending unprofitable, credit contracts, and recession becomes self-fulfilling. Every US recession since 1968 (8 of 8) was preceded by a sustained inversion, zero misses, leading by 12-18 months on average. Because the lead is long (markets rallied 15 more months after the 2006 inversion; the 2022 inversion was followed by the AI bull), it is unfit as an exit signal — its only role here: when inverted for ≥3 months (63 trading days) it vetoes entry into the attack tier (downgraded to neutral; no maximum leverage while the recession clock is confirmed ticking). It never triggers defense and is not a lock.
hints.epuTrade: Rule: trade policy uncertainty index (monthly, trade/tariff/tech-war specific) 10-year percentile >80 → tight, <50 → loose; must agree with daily EPU, else neutral
hints.reference: Reference only, not part of signal judgment
hints.epuDaily: Rule: daily economic policy uncertainty index 7-day MA (news-based; reacts within days to policy pivots like tariff pauses) 10-year percentile >80 → tight, <50 → loose; must agree with the monthly trade index, else neutral
hints.oilWti: WTI 30-day change is the market's real-time pricing of war/geopolitical shocks, taking priority over EPU percentiles: gain ≥+20% AND EPU elevated (>80th pct) → tight (war/supply shock); a surge while EPU is calm = demand recovery, not misread as defense. Drop ≥20% AND EPU not elevated → loose (de-escalation); if EPU is simultaneously high the crash is crisis-type — no loose.
hints.fiscalOutlaysTtm: Rule (small-government): TTM federal outlays YoY expands >5% → tight (government growing, harmful to markets); shrinks >5% → loose (government slimming). Why outlays not deficit: deficit mixes in the revenue side — tax-cut-driven deficits (e.g. 2017) fit the low-tax principle and should not read as tight. Measured as real (inflation-adjusted) outlays YoY: nominal outlays naturally grow ~5% and would chronically pre-trigger tight; after PCE deflation it only fires when real expansion/contraction exceeds ±5%

indicators.hintGlobal: Global rule: Attack (asymmetric) = AI supply/demand loose (the active engine firing) + monetary/fiscal/admin all not tight (neutral is fine; any tight vetoes) + no locks + yield curve not in confirmed inversion (10y−3m inverted ≥3 months / 63 trading days vetoes attack entry, downgrading to watch); one dimension tight = reduce & watch — stop adding, raise alertness; no need to trim existing positions (backtest evidence: months with a single tight dimension average market returns no lower than other months — a single tight is an attention signal, not an action signal); two+ tight or Sahm/reactive lock active = full defense. Trend re-entry exception: when the market is in an uptrend (latest close ≥ SMA of the last 10 month-end closes), decision-tree-driven full defense is downgraded to reduce & watch; lock-driven defense is never overridden by the trend. Downgrade hysteresis: upgrades (more defensive, incl. lock-forced) take effect immediately; downgrades (more lenient) require the milder tier to persist for a 30-day confirmation period, keeping the previous tier meanwhile
indicators.staleHint: Data source temporarily unavailable; last valid judgment retained

watchlist.title: Watchlist
watchlist.percentileHint: Position of current price within all closes of the selected range. Rule: ≥80% high (red, euphoria — be cautious / consider selling); ≤20% low (green, neglected — watch for buying); 20–80% mid (yellow, wait). 99% = near range high, 10% = near range low
watchlist.valuationHint: ETFs/indexes have no financial statements, so blank P/E and P/S is normal; for individual stocks, blanks usually mean a temporary data-source rate limit — refresh later

interpret.title: Signal Interpretation
interpret.toAttack: Toward attack (AI supply/demand loose + monetary/fiscal/admin not tight + no locks + yield curve not in confirmed inversion):
interpret.allLoose: Attack conditions met: AI supply/demand loose (active engine firing), monetary/fiscal/admin not tight, no locks, yield curve not in confirmed inversion
interpret.yieldCurveOk: Yield curve not in confirmed inversion

recessionLock.waitCondition: Unlock: rate down to 0-0.25% (zero-rate zone — unconditional, not subject to the minimum lock age); or a <50bp small rate adjustment once the lock is at least 60 days old and the trigger condition has cleared that day

admin.title: Admin Panel
admin.lockOverrideNote: Emergency valve: manually clear if data anomaly caused a false trigger

error.fetchFailed: Failed to load data, showing cached results

aiChain.title: AI Industry Chain Money Flow
aiChain.bubbleWarning: Bubble warning: AI demand indicators weakening, consider defense

track.title: Public Signal Track Record
track.subtitle: Daily signal tiers are archived here immutably for anyone to verify. Backtest methodology and full report: docs/backtest-report.md in the GitHub repo. For research reference only, not investment advice.
track.stratCagr: Strategy CAGR (exit on full defense only)
track.btNote: Based on a {years}-year monthly-replay backtest; past performance does not guarantee future results.
